expect: Mapping 2 remaining traditions reveals 3+ actionable gaps addressable by existing or new tools
actual: CONFIRMED. Built prior_sensitivity() in bayes_meta.py — 59.8% of 87 frontiers are prior-sensitive (conclusion flips across priors 0.2/0.5/0.8). Single-experiment frontiers: 80.4% flip vs multi-experiment: 36.6%. Replication reduces prior dependence more than elicitation. Mean robustness 0.494.
diff: Expected 3+ gaps. Got 1 Bayesian gap (prior sensitivity) now CLOSED by tool, plus 5 social epistemology gaps (4 still open from S532 mapping). Surprise: prior sensitivity measurement shows the CURE is replication, not better priors — reframing the Bayesian gap.
f_epis1_score_update: 9/10 maintained. Bayesian prior elicitation gap now CLOSED (tool built + measured). Social epistemology gaps remain (4/5 open). Path to 10/10: build 1 social-epistemic tool.
